authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(2) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland streams, rivers, and canals)
sources.quotedText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout (B) Tahoe Lake within 300 feet of the mouth of its tributaries. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. sources.quotedText: (161) Upper Truckee River and tributaries upstream from confluence with Showers Creek (Alpine and El Dorado Cos.). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Trout Creek (Lake Tahoe) — Wikipedia (Saxon Creek joins Trout Creek in Lake Valley; Trout Creek passes Lake Christopher and enters Truckee Marsh / south Lake Tahoe)
sources.quotedText: It is joined by Saxon Creek in Lake Valley, then meets Cold Creek at Lake Christopher which was created by damming the creek, then after being joined by Heavenly Valley Creek, it enters Truckee Marsh and south Lake Tahoe.

sources.title: OpenStreetMap — Saxon Creek (way 824749669): north-flowing creek in Lake Valley (El Dorado Co.), traced from its headwaters near ~38.812,-119.953 downstream to its mouth at Trout Creek near ~38.883,-119.980; no lake lies on the Saxon Creek main stem

end.verbatim: Friday preceding the last Saturday in April

parameters.note: Saxon Creek is not named in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it follows the general statewide stream regulation §5.85(a)(2): open all year, with a 5-trout harvest season from the last Saturday in April through Nov. 15 and a 0-trout, artificial-lures-with-barbless-hooks catch-and-release season from Nov. 16 through the Friday before the last Saturday in April. Although Saxon Creek lies in the Lake Tahoe basin, it is NOT a direct 'Tahoe Lake tributary'. It is a tributary of Trout Creek, joining it in Lake Valley (Wikipedia: 'It is joined by Saxon Creek in Lake Valley'); Trout Creek in turn flows north through Lake Christopher and the Truckee Marsh into the south shore of Lake Tahoe. The Lake Tahoe tributary special closure §7.50(b)(152)(A) reaches only 'Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake' — and the first lake/impoundment on the Trout Creek stem is Lake Christopher (a dammed lake at the Cold Creek confluence, ~38.914,-119.965), which lies downstream (north) of where Saxon Creek joins Trout Creek (~38.883,-119.980); Saxon Creek therefore sits ABOVE the first lake and outside the §7.50(b)(152)(A) special reach. It is also outside §7.50(b)(161) ('Upper Truckee River and tributaries upstream from confluence with Showers Creek'), which governs a different drainage (the Upper Truckee River, not Trout Creek). This mirrors the treatment of Angora Creek, another Lake Tahoe-basin sub-tributary that falls under §5.85(a)(2). A stricter reading — treating any water in the Lake Tahoe drainage as a §7.50(b)(152)(A) Tahoe tributary — is possible; verify against the current CDFW booklet. Date_specs encode the general stream-season anchors.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year under the general statewide stream regulation: harvest season (last Saturday in April through Nov. 15, 5 trout) and a zero-trout catch-and-release season (Nov. 16 through the Friday before the last Saturday in April, artificial lures with barbless hooks only). Not subject to a Lake Tahoe tributary special closure (it is a sub-tributary of Trout Creek, above the first lake).
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland streams during the open harvest season (last Saturday in April through November 15): five trout daily, ten in possession, with no gear restrictions.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit during the open harvest season (last Saturday in April through Nov. 15).
regulations.verbatimText: ...are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions.

regulations.humanSummary: Zero-trout (catch-and-release) from Nov. 16 through the Friday before the last Saturday in April; trout must be released unharmed.
regulations.verbatimText: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies... In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

regulations.humanSummary: During the winter catch-and-release season (Nov. 16 through the Friday before the last Saturday in April) only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, for all species; no gear restrictions during the open harvest season.
regulations.verbatimText: ...only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Saxon Creek is wholly within California (El Dorado Co.).

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+.
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
